title: Night Shift
content:
ï¿½Hello! thatï¿½s the whistle, be moving.
Wake up! donï¿½t lie muttering there.
What language! your style is improving,
Itï¿½s pleasant to hear you at prayer.
Turn out, man, and spare us the blessing.
Cribï¿½s cut, and the teaï¿½s on the brew.
Youï¿½ll have to look slippy in dressing
For that was the half-hour that blew.ï¿½

ï¿½Half-past! and the nightï¿½s simply awful,
The hut fairly shakes in the storm.
Hang night-shifts! They shouldnï¿½t be lawful;
Iï¿½ve only had time to get warm.
I notice the hutï¿½s rarely bright, and
The bunkï¿½s always cold as a stone,
Except when I go on at night, and
The half-after whistles have blown.

ï¿½Bob built up that fire just to spite me,
The conscienceless son of a swab!
By Jove! it would fairly delight me
To let Hogan be hanged with his job.
Oh! itï¿½s easy to preach of contentment;
Youï¿½re eloquent all on the flute.
Old Nickï¿½s everlasting resentment
Plague Dick if heï¿½s taken my boot!

ï¿½Great Cï¿½sar! you roasted the liquor,
Whoever it was made the tea;
Itï¿½s hotter than hell-broth and thicker!
Fried bacon again. Not for me!
Good night, and be hanged! Stir up, Stumpy,
You look very happy and warm;
Iï¿½ll hoist half the bark off the humpy
And give you a taste of the storm.ï¿½

We laughed as he went away growling:
But down where the wind whipped the creek
The storm like old fury was howling,
And Fred was on top for the week.
ï¿½A devilï¿½s own night for the braceman,ï¿½
Muttered Con. ï¿½Itï¿½s a comfort to know
All weathers are one to the faceman,
All shifts are alike down below.ï¿½

We slept, and the storm was receding,
The wind moaned a dirge overhead,
When men brought him, broken and bleeding,
And laid him again on the bed.
We saw by the flame burning dimly
The gray hue of death on his face.
The stoker enlightened us grimly:
ï¿½No hope. He was blown from the brace.ï¿½
meaning:
This evocative piece by Edward Dyson represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance, inviting the reader to reflect on the deeper themes of human experience and artistic expression.

In the broader context of Edward Dyson's bibliography, these specific lines stand out for their clarity and poignancy. Whether you are searching for inspiration or a moment of quiet contemplation, this work offers a timeless perspective that transcends its original era.

At Linespedia, we have curated this specific poem to ensure it reaches a modern audience. We recommend sharing these lines as a digital poster or using them as a thoughtful caption to connect with others who appreciate the enduring beauty of verse.
